Title: Using Self-Organizing Networks in Public Policy
Description: SON technology will ease the increasing complexity and heterogeneity of LTE networks. IFT, Mexico’s telecom regulator, believes that SON is also a powerful tool for public policy design. Leading the world on the way to this paradigm shift, IFT has come up with several innovative approaches centered around SON:   -cross-border interference mitigation through SON   -dynamic capacity allocation to Public Safety institutions through commercial LTE networks   - detection of unwanted RF emissions, recognition of type of sources, jamming   - seamless integration into commercial networks of movable base stations for rescue and law enforcement operations

Title: Adoption of the APT segmentation of the 700 MHz band in Mexico
Description: In 2012, I made a study of the cross-border spectrum co-existence between US and Mexico and the comparative study of LTE network deployment in Mexico City. (see Annex 1 and 6 by following the link). This turned out to be a major input to the country's decision to adopt the APT band plan for the future use of the band of the 700 MHz band. This triggered the further adoption of the band by many other countries and created a multi-billion people market for telecommunications equipment.

Title: Wikipedia article on the 700 MHz band
MercadoLibre (the biggest eCommerce player in Latin America, NASDAQ:MELI): big data analytics for user&revenue growth and customer insights
Description: Our team applied big data analytics to arrive at customer insights and user&revenue  growth strategy for peer to peer mobile payments platform for Latin American markets (think Braintree+Venmo+Square). We interviewed 30 people in person, crafted and launched surveys with 5000+ responses and subsequently clustered the customers into segments. We then proceeded to create an aggressive marketing plan. Our big data analysis also revealed a significant economic fraud for which we calculated the impact and exposed the method.

Title: Volaris (a major North American airline, NYSE:VLRS): 1) Customer segmentation and product recommendations through big data analytics; 2) non-flight travel offers strategy
Description: I am currently working on two major projects in the strategy department of the airline. For the first project, I am applying big data analytics to all customer data of the airline in order to come up with business insights and new projects in terms of eMarketing, promotions, expansion, non-ticket revenue generation, product recommendations, etc. The second project is regarding the non-flight travel offers: hotels, rentals, packages, etc.

Title: Shanghai’s Anting Municipality administration: precision medicine ecosystem buildout strategy
Description: Our team identified a pathway for Anting to shift current focus on automotive industry to precision medicine. We created a set of recommendations and a roadmap on how to create a precision medicine ecosystem in Shanghai, attract best tenants and talent, create a brand, and what incentives to put in place.

Title: MIT Future of Communication Program: research on the business implications of the evolution of video over IP.
Description: My research focused on evolution of video over IP. When companies like YouTube, Netflix or Comcast announce new features, it is often hard to make sense of the overall industry trends and know what technologies or practices are ought to become industry standards , or so called dominant designs, and which are to be soon disconnected and forgotten. I collected primary data on such announcements for the past 10 years and analyzed them in order to come up with a tool for decision-makers to make sense of the changes in the industry.
